title: My Daily Routine
arabic: كِل يَوم بِفيق السَّاعة سَبعة الصُّبح. بِغسِل وِجّي وبِنزِل عَالمَطبَخ. بِعمِل فِنجان قَهوة وبَاكُل صَحن لَبنة مَع خُبز. بَعدين بِطلَع عَالشُّغل بِالسَّيّارة. بِالنهار بِتغَدّى بِالمَكتَب. بِرجَع عَالبيت السَّاعة سِتّة وبِقعُد مَع عيلتي. بِالليل بِتعَشّى وبِتفَرَّج عَالتِّلفزيون وبنام السَّاعة حدَعش.
transliteration: Kill yōm bfīʔ is-sēʕa sabʕa iṣ-ṣubiḥ. Bghissil wijji w binzil ʕal-maṭbakh. Baʕmil finjēn ʔahwe w bēkul ṣaḥn labne maʕ khubz. Baʕdēn biṭlaʕ ʕash-shughul bis-sayyāra. Bin-nhār bitghadda bil-maktab. Birjaʕ ʕal-bēt is-sēʕa sitte w buʔʕud maʕ ʕēlti. Bil-lēl bitʕashsha w bitfarraj ʕat-tilfizyōn w bnēm is-sēʕa ḥdaʕsh.
english: Every day I wake up at seven in the morning. I wash my face and go down to the kitchen. I make a cup of coffee and eat a plate of labneh with bread. Then I go to work by car. At noon I have lunch at the office. I come back home at six and sit with my family. At night I have dinner, watch TV, and sleep at eleven.

comprehensionQuestions.question: What time does the narrator wake up?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: At seven in the morning (الساعة سبعة الصبح)

comprehensionQuestions.question: What does the narrator eat for breakfast?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Labneh with bread and coffee

comprehensionQuestions.question: How does the narrator get to work?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: By car (بالسيارة)

comprehensionQuestions.question: What does the narrator do at night?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Has dinner, watches TV, and sleeps at eleven

title: At the Cafe
arabic: أَنا وصاحبتي مِنروح عَالكافيه كِل يَوم جُمعة. مِنقعُد بَرّا إذا الجَوّ حِلو. بِطلُب قَهوة تُركِيّة وهِيّي بِتطلُب عَصير لَيمون. كَمان مِنّاكُل مَنقوشة زَعتَر أَو كنافة. مِنحكي كتير ومِنضحَك سَوا. الكافيه حِلو ومِرتاح والنَّاس كِلّا لطيفة هون.
transliteration: Ana w ṣāḥibti mnirūḥ ʕal-kāfēh kill yōm jumʕa. Mnuʔʕud barra iza ij-jaww ḥilw. Buṭlub ʔahwe turkiyye w hiyye btitlub ʕaṣīr lēmūn. Kamēn mnēkul manʔūshe zaʕtar aw knēfe. Mniḥki ktīr w minḍaḥḥak sawa. Il-kāfēh ḥilw w mirtēḥ win-nās killa laṭīfe hōn.
english: My friend and I go to the cafe every Friday. We sit outside if the weather is nice. I order Turkish coffee and she orders lemon juice. We also eat a zaatar manoushe or knafeh. We talk a lot and laugh together. The cafe is nice and comfortable and the people are all friendly here.

comprehensionQuestions.question: When do they go to the cafe?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Every Friday (كل يوم جمعة)

comprehensionQuestions.question: Where do they sit?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Outside, if the weather is nice

comprehensionQuestions.question: What does the narrator's friend order?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Lemon juice (عصير ليمون)

comprehensionQuestions.question: How does the narrator describe the cafe?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Nice and comfortable with friendly people

title: A Day at the Souk
arabic: كل يوم سبت، أنا وأمي منروح عالسوق. الزحمة كتير بس الجو حلو. أول شي منفوت عند أبو سليم، يلي عندو أحسن خضرا بكل المنطقة. بيقلنا: تفضلو، اليوم عنا بندورة طازة من البقاع! بعدين منروح عند الفران ومنجيب خبز ومناقيش زعتر. آخر شي، منوقف عند بسطة العصير ومنشرب ليمونادة باردة. هيدا أحلى جزء من نهار السبت.
transliteration: Kill yōm sabt, ana w immi mnirūḥ ʕas-sūʔ. Iz-zaḥme ktīr bas ij-jaww ḥilw. Awwal shi mnifūt ʕand abu Slēm, yalli ʕandu aḥsan khaḍra b-kill il-manṭaʔa. Byiʔillna: tafaḍḍalu, il-yōm ʕanna banadūra ṭāza min il-Biʔāʕ! Baʕdēn mnirūḥ ʕand il-farrān w mnijīb khubz w manāʔīsh zaʕtar. Ākhir shi, mnūʔaf ʕand basṭit il-ʕaṣīr w mnishrab lēmōnāda bārde. Hayda aḥla juzʔ min nhār is-sabt.
english: Every Saturday, my mom and I go to the market. It's very crowded but the weather is nice. First, we go to Abu Sleim's place, who has the best vegetables in the whole area. He tells us: Welcome, today we have fresh tomatoes from the Bekaa! Then we go to the bakery and get bread and zaatar manoushe. Last thing, we stop at the juice stand and drink cold lemonade. This is the best part of Saturday.

comprehensionQuestions.question: When do they go to the market?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Every Saturday (كل يوم سبت)

comprehensionQuestions.question: What is special about Abu Sleim?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: He has the best vegetables in the area

comprehensionQuestions.question: Where are the tomatoes from?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: The Bekaa Valley (البقاع)

comprehensionQuestions.question: What do they drink at the end?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Cold lemonade (ليمونادة باردة)

title: Teta's Kitchen
arabic: تيتا كانت أحسن طباخة بالعيلة. كل يوم أحد، كنا نروح عبيتا ونقعد بالمطبخ نتفرج عليا وهي عم تطبخ. كانت تقلنا: تعو ساعدوني! يلا قشرو البطاطا. ريحة الأكل كانت توصل لعند الجيران. أول شي كانت تعمل التبولة — بقدونس، بندورة، برغل، وحامض. بعدين الكبة — لحمة مع برغل وبصل. وآخر شي المغربية. كنا نقعد كلنا سوا عالطاولة الكبيرة ومنحكي ومناكل ومنضحك. هيدي أحلى ذكريات عندي.
transliteration: Tēta kānit aḥsan ṭabbākha bil-ʕēle. Kill yōm aḥad, kunna nirūḥ ʕa-bēta w nuʔʕud bil-maṭbakh nitfarraj ʕalēha w hiyye ʕam tiṭbukh. Kānit tiʔillna: taʕo sāʕdūni! Yalla ʔashru il-baṭāṭa. Rīḥit il-akl kānit tūṣal la-ʕand ij-jīrān. Awwal shi kānit taʕmil it-tabbūle — baʔdūnis, banadūra, burghul, w ḥāmuḍ. Baʕdēn il-kibbe — laḥme maʕ burghul w baṣal. W ākhir shi il-mghrabiyye. Kunna nuʔʕud killna sawa ʕaṭ-ṭāwle il-kbīre w minḥki w mnēkul w mindaḥḥak. Haydi aḥla zikriyāt ʕandī.
english: Teta was the best cook in the family. Every Sunday, we would go to her house and sit in the kitchen watching her cook. She would tell us: Come help me! Let's go, peel the potatoes. The smell of the food would reach the neighbors. First she would make tabbouleh — parsley, tomatoes, bulgur, and lemon. Then kibbeh — meat with bulgur and onion. And last, moghrabieh. We would all sit together at the big table and talk and eat and laugh. These are my best memories.

comprehensionQuestions.question: When did the family visit Teta?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Every Sunday (كل يوم أحد)

comprehensionQuestions.question: What did Teta ask the kids to do?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Help her and peel the potatoes

comprehensionQuestions.question: Name the three dishes Teta made.
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Tabbouleh, kibbeh, and moghrabieh

comprehensionQuestions.question: What happened at the big table?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: They all sat together, talked, ate, and laughed

title: My Neighborhood in Beirut
arabic: أنا ساكن بحي الحمرا ببيروت. الحي كتير حلو ومليان حياة. عند ناصية البيت في دكانة أبو حسن — هيدا الزلمة بيعرف كل واحد بالحي بالإسم. صبحية بكير، بيفتح الدكانة وبيحط الفواكه والخضرا برّا. بروح عندو كل يوم بجيب قهوة وجريدة. الشارع الرئيسي فيو مطاعم وكافيهات وبنوك. بالليل، الزحمة بتخف والناس بتقعد بالكافيهات بتشرب قهوة وبتحكي. أحلى وقت هو بالربيع لما الجو بيكون دافي والشمس بتنزل حلوة.
transliteration: Ana sākin b-ḥayy il-Ḥamra b-Bēyrūt. Il-ḥayy ktīr ḥilw w malyān ḥayā. ʕand nāṣyit il-bēt fī dikkānit abu Ḥasan — hayda iz-zalame byaʕrif kill wāḥad bil-ḥayy bil-isim. Ṣubḥiyye bakkīr, byiftaḥ id-dikkāne w byiḥuṭṭ il-fawākih wil-khaḍra barra. Brūḥ ʕandu kill yōm bjīb ʔahwe w jarīde. Ish-shāriʕ ir-raʔīsi fī maṭāʕim w kāfīhāt w bnūk. Bil-lēl, iz-zaḥme btkhiff win-nās btuʔʕud bil-kāfīhāt btishrab ʔahwe w btiḥki. Aḥla waʔt huwwe bir-rabīʕ lamma ij-jaww byikūn dāfi wish-shams btinzal ḥilwe.
english: I live in the Hamra neighborhood in Beirut. The neighborhood is very nice and full of life. At the corner of the building is Abu Hassan's shop — this man knows everyone in the neighborhood by name. Early morning, he opens the shop and puts the fruits and vegetables outside. I go to him every day to get coffee and a newspaper. The main street has restaurants, cafes, and banks. At night, the traffic calms down and people sit in cafes drinking coffee and chatting. The best time is in spring when the weather is warm and the sun is nice.

comprehensionQuestions.question: Where does the narrator live?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: In the Hamra neighborhood in Beirut

comprehensionQuestions.question: What does Abu Hassan know?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Everyone in the neighborhood by name

comprehensionQuestions.question: What does the narrator buy daily?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Coffee and a newspaper

comprehensionQuestions.question: What is the best time according to the narrator?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Spring, when the weather is warm

title: A Family Story
arabic: الأَحَد الماضي إجتَمَعِت كِل العيلة عِند بيت عَمّي. تيتا حَضَّرِت مَحشي وتَبّولة، وعَمّي شَوى لَحمة عَالمَنقَل. الأَولاد كانو يِلعبو بِالجَنينة، وخالتي كانِت عَم تحكي مَع أُمّي عَن أَيّام زَمان. جَدّو قِعِد عَالكِرسي وبَلَّش يِحكيلنا قِصّة عَن لَمّا كان صغير بِالضيعة. كِلّنا ضحِكنا كتير لَمّا حَكى كيف وِقِع بِالنِّهر. بِالآخِر، قَعَدنا كِلّنا سَوا ومناكُل بَقلاوة وشرِبنا قَهوة. هَيدي أَحلى لَيالي بِحَياتنا.
transliteration: Il-aḥad il-māḍi jtamaʕit kill il-ʕēle ʕand bēt ʕammi. Tēta ḥaḍḍarit maḥshi w tabbūle, w ʕammi shawa laḥme ʕal-manʔal. Il-awlēd kānu yilʕabu bil-jnēne, w khālti kānit ʕam tiḥki maʕ immi ʕan ayyām zamān. Jiddo ʔiʕid ʕal-kirsi w ballash yiḥkīlna ʔiṣṣa ʕan lamma kān zghīr biḍ-ḍēʕa. Killna ḍḥikna ktīr lamma ḥaka kīf wiʔiʕ bin-nihir. Bil-ākhir, ʔaʕadna killna sawa w mnēkul baʔlēwa w shribna ʔahwe. Haydi aḥla layāli b-ḥayātna.
english: Last Sunday the whole family gathered at my uncle's house. Teta prepared stuffed grape leaves and tabbouleh, and my uncle grilled meat on the barbecue. The kids were playing in the garden, and my aunt was talking with my mom about the old days. Grandpa sat in his chair and started telling us a story about when he was young in the village. We all laughed a lot when he told us how he fell in the river. At the end, we all sat together and ate baklava and drank coffee. These are the best nights of our lives.

comprehensionQuestions.question: When did the family gathering happen?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Last Sunday (الأحد الماضي)

comprehensionQuestions.question: What did Teta prepare?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Stuffed grape leaves (محشي) and tabbouleh

comprehensionQuestions.question: What story did Grandpa tell?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: About when he was young and fell in the river

comprehensionQuestions.question: What did they eat and drink at the end?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Baklava and coffee

title: A Trip to the South
arabic: الصَّيف الماضي رُحنا أَنا وأَصحابي رِحلة عَالجَنوب. طِلعنا مِن بيروت الصُّبح وسُقنا عَالأَوتوستراد. الطَّريق كانِت حِلوة كتير — مِن هون بَحر، ومِن هون جِبال خَضرا. أَوَّل شي وَقَّفنا بِصَيدا وتمَشَّينا بِالسوق القَديم. بَعدين كَمَّلنا عَصور وشُفنا القَلعة عَالبَحر. أَكَلنا سَمَك مَشوي بِمَطعَم صغير عَالشَّط. بَعد الغَدا رُحنا عَقَلعة بوفور وشُفنا مَنظَر خيالي مِن فوق. رجِعنا عَبيروت بِالليل تَعبانين بَس مَبسوطين كتير.
transliteration: Iṣ-ṣēf il-māḍi ruḥna ana w aṣḥābi riḥle ʕal-janūb. Ṭliʕna min Bēyrūt iṣ-ṣubiḥ w suʔna ʕal-ōtōstrād. Iṭ-ṭarīʔ kānit ḥilwe ktīr — min hōn baḥar, w min hōn jbāl khaḍra. Awwal shi waʔʔafna b-Ṣayda w tmashshēna bis-sūʔ il-ʔadīm. Baʕdēn kammalna ʕa-Ṣūr w shufna il-ʔalʕa ʕal-baḥar. Akalna samak mashwi b-maṭʕam zghīr ʕash-shaṭṭ. Baʕd il-ghada ruḥna ʕa-ʔalʕit Bōfōr w shufna manẓar khayāli min fōʔ. Rjiʕna ʕa-Bēyrūt bil-lēl taʕbānīn bas mabsūṭīn ktīr.
english: Last summer my friends and I went on a trip to the South. We left Beirut in the morning and drove on the highway. The road was very beautiful — on one side the sea, and on the other green mountains. First we stopped in Sidon and walked around the old market. Then we continued to Tyre and saw the castle by the sea. We ate grilled fish at a small restaurant on the beach. After lunch we went to Beaufort Castle and saw an incredible view from the top. We came back to Beirut at night, tired but very happy.

comprehensionQuestions.question: When did the trip happen?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Last summer (الصيف الماضي)

comprehensionQuestions.question: What did they see on the road?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: The sea on one side and green mountains on the other

comprehensionQuestions.question: What two cities did they visit?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Sidon (صيدا) and Tyre (صور)

comprehensionQuestions.question: How did they feel coming back?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Tired but very happy (تعبانين بس مبسوطين كتير)

title: The Lebanese Diaspora
arabic: في لبنانيين بكل مكان بالعالم. من البرازيل لأستراليا، من أفريقيا لأميركا — وين ما تروح، رح تلاقي لبناني فاتح مطعم أو دكانة أو شركة. الغربة مش سهلة. بتفكر ببلدك كل يوم، بتشتاق لريحة الزعتر الصبح وصوت أمك وهي عم تنده عليك. بس اللبناني ما بيتكسر. بيشتغل وبيبني حياة جديدة وبيبقى يحلم بالرجعة. أكتر شي بيوجع هو لما تسمع أغنية فيروز وإنت بعيد. دمعة بتنزل وبتقول: يا ريت كنت هونيك. بس بعدين بتمسح الدمعة وبتكمل. هيدا اللبناني — بيضحك وبيبكي بنفس الوقت.
transliteration: Fī libnāniyyīn b-kill makān bil-ʕālam. Min il-Brazīl la-Ustrālya, min Afrīqya la-Amērka — wēn ma trūḥ, raḥ tlāʔi libnāni fātiḥ maṭʕam aw dikkāne aw sharike. Il-ghurbe mish sahle. Btifakkir b-baldak kill yōm, btishta' la-rīḥit iz-zaʕtar iṣ-ṣubiḥ w ṣōt immak w hiyye ʕam tindah ʕalēk. Bas il-libnāni ma byitkassar. Byishtaghil w byibni ḥayā jdīde w byibʔa yiḥlam bir-rajʕa. Aktar shi byūjaʕ huwwe lamma tismaʕ ughniyit Fayrūz w inta bʕīd. Damʕa btinzal w btiʔūl: ya rēt kint honīk. Bas baʕdēn btimṣaḥ id-damʕa w btikammil. Hayda il-libnāni — byiḍḥak w byibki b-nafs il-waʔt.
english: There are Lebanese people everywhere in the world. From Brazil to Australia, from Africa to America — wherever you go, you'll find a Lebanese person who opened a restaurant or a shop or a company. Being away from home isn't easy. You think about your country every day, you miss the smell of zaatar in the morning and your mother's voice calling for you. But the Lebanese person doesn't break. They work and build a new life and keep dreaming of going back. The thing that hurts most is when you hear a Fairouz song and you're far away. A tear falls and you say: I wish I was there. But then you wipe the tear and continue. That's the Lebanese — laughing and crying at the same time.

comprehensionQuestions.question: Where can you find Lebanese people?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Everywhere in the world — from Brazil to Australia to Africa to America

comprehensionQuestions.question: What does the narrator miss most?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: The smell of zaatar in the morning and their mother's voice

comprehensionQuestions.question: What happens when you hear a Fairouz song abroad?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: A tear falls and you wish you were back home

comprehensionQuestions.question: How does the narrator describe the Lebanese person?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Laughing and crying at the same time — they don't break

title: The Village in Summer
arabic: كل صيف، كنا نطلع عالضيعة. الضيعة بالجبل، الهوا نضيف والسما أقرب. بيت جدو كان قديم، حجر أصفر وباب خشب كبير. كنا نوصل وتيتا تكون واقفة عالباب: أهلا يا حبايبي! وينكن من زمان! كنا نقعد تحت التينة الكبيرة، نشرب قهوة عربية ونسمع جدو يحكيلنا قصص عن زمان. بالعصرونية، كنا نروح نمشي بالوادي، نقطف تين وعنب من الكروم. بالليل، كنا نقعد عالبلكون ونتطلع عالنجوم. ما كان في إنترنت ولا تلفونات — بس حكي ودحك وقهوة. يا ريت هالأيام بيرجعو.
transliteration: Kill ṣēf, kunna niṭlaʕ ʕaḍ-ḍēʕa. Iḍ-ḍēʕa bil-jabal, il-hawa nḍīf wis-sama aʔrab. Bēt jiddo kān ʔadīm, ḥajar aṣfar w bāb khashab kbīr. Kunna nūṣal w tēta tkūn wāʔfe ʕal-bāb: ahla ya ḥabāybi! Wēnkun min zamān! Kunna nuʔʕud taḥt it-tīne il-kbīre, nishrab ʔahwe ʕarabiyye w nismaʕ jiddo yiḥkīlna ʔiṣaṣ ʕan zamān. Bil-ʕaṣrūniyye, kunna nrūḥ nimshi bil-wādi, niʔṭuf tīn w ʕinab min il-krūm. Bil-lēl, kunna nuʔʕud ʕal-balkōn w niṭṭallaʕ ʕan-njūm. Ma kān fī internet wala talfūnāt — bas ḥaki w daḥḥak w ʔahwe. Ya rēt hal-ayyām byirjaʕu.
english: Every summer, we would go up to the village. The village is in the mountains, the air is clean and the sky is closer. Grandpa's house was old, yellow stone with a big wooden door. We would arrive and Teta would be standing at the door: Welcome my darlings! Where have you been so long! We would sit under the big fig tree, drink Arabic coffee and listen to Grandpa tell us stories about the old days. In the afternoon, we would go walk in the valley, picking figs and grapes from the vineyards. At night, we would sit on the balcony and look at the stars. There was no internet or phones — just talking and laughing and coffee. I wish those days would come back.

comprehensionQuestions.question: Where is the village?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: In the mountains (بالجبل)

comprehensionQuestions.question: What was Teta doing when they arrived?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Standing at the door to welcome them

comprehensionQuestions.question: What did they do under the fig tree?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Drink Arabic coffee and listen to Grandpa's stories

comprehensionQuestions.question: What does the narrator wish at the end?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: That those days would come back (يا ريت هالأيام بيرجعو)

title: The Taxi Driver
arabic: ركبت سرفيس من الحمرا لعالأشرفية. السواق كان زلمة كبير بالعمر، شعرو أبيض وإيدو عالراديو. أول ما ركبت قلي: منين إنت يا ابني؟ قلتلو: من أميركا بس أصلي لبناني. ضحك وقال: والله؟ إنت لبناني ومش عارف تحكي عربي؟ قلتلو: لا، بحكي شوي. قال: يلا احكي، خبرني عن حالك. وصرنا نحكي كل الطريق. حكالي عن زمان بيروت، عن الحرب، عن ولادو يلي سافرو. قلتلو: كيف بتقدر تبقى متفائل؟ قال: يا ابني، اللبناني ما عندو خيار. بيضحك لأنو إذا ما ضحك، بيبكي. وصلني عالأشرفية ورفض ياخد مصاري. قال: هيدي من عندي — أهلا فيك ببلدك.
transliteration: Rkibt sarvīs min il-Ḥamra la-ʕal-Ashrafiyye. Is-sawwāʔ kān zalame kbīr bil-ʕumr, shaʕro abyaḍ w ēdu ʕar-rādyo. Awwal ma rkibt ʔalli: mnēn inta ya ibni? ʔultillu: min Amērka bas aṣli libnāni. Ḍiḥik w ʔāl: wallah? Inta libnāni w mish ʕārif tiḥki ʕarabi? ʔultillu: la, biḥki shwayy. ʔāl: yalla iḥki, khabirni ʕan ḥālak. W ṣirna niḥki kill iṭ-ṭarīʔ. Ḥakāli ʕan zamān Bēyrūt, ʕan il-ḥarb, ʕan wlādu yalli sāfru. ʔultillu: kīf btiʔdar tibʔa mitfāʔil? ʔāl: ya ibni, il-libnāni ma ʕandu khiyār. Byiḍḥak laʔannu iza ma ḍiḥik, byibki. Waṣṣalni ʕal-Ashrafiyye w rafaḍ yākhud maṣāri. ʔāl: haydi min ʕandi — ahla fīk b-baldak.
english: I took a shared taxi from Hamra to Ashrafieh. The driver was an older man, white hair, hand on the radio. As soon as I got in he said: Where are you from, son? I told him: From America, but originally Lebanese. He laughed and said: Really? You're Lebanese and you don't know how to speak Arabic? I told him: No, I speak a little. He said: Go ahead, talk. Tell me about yourself. And we talked the whole way. He told me about old Beirut, about the war, about his children who emigrated. I asked him: How do you stay optimistic? He said: Son, the Lebanese person has no choice. He laughs because if he doesn't laugh, he cries. He dropped me off in Ashrafieh and refused to take money. He said: This one's on me — welcome to your country.

comprehensionQuestions.question: Where was the narrator going?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: From Hamra to Ashrafieh

comprehensionQuestions.question: What surprised the driver?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: That the narrator is Lebanese but wasn't sure about speaking Arabic

comprehensionQuestions.question: What did the driver say about Lebanese people?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: They laugh because if they don't laugh, they cry

comprehensionQuestions.question: Why did the driver refuse money?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: He welcomed the narrator to his country — it was a gesture of hospitality

title: A Diaspora Reflection
arabic: صار إلي عَشر سنين بَرّا لبنان، وكِل يَوم بِحِس إنّو في شي ناقِص جُوّاتي. لَمّا فِتِّت أَوَّل مَرّة عَالمَطار، كِنت فَكَّرت إنّها رِحلة قَصيرة — بِتروح وبِترجع. بَس الأَيّام صارِت شهور، والشهور سنين، وإنت عَم تِنسى شكل شوارع بلدك. أَصعب شي هُوّي لَمّا حَدا يسألني: مِنَين إنت؟ لأنّو الجَواب ما عاد بَسيط. أَنا مِن لبنان بَس عايِش هون. لبناني بِالقَلب بَس حَياتي هون. بِاللّيل، بِسمَع فَيروز وبِحِس إنّو صَوتا عَم يسحَبني عَالوَرا — عَجَبَل صنّين، عَساحة الشُّهَدا، عَريحة المانجا بِصَيدا. بِكتُب شِعر بِاللّيل عَن بلَد ما عاد بِشبِه البلد يلي تَرَكتو. بِعرِف إنّو لبنان تغَيَّر، وأَنا كَمان تغَيَّرت. بَس الحَنين ما بيتغَيَّر — هَيدا الشّي الوَحيد الثَّابِت بِحَياتي.
transliteration: Ṣār illi ʕashr snīn barra Libnān, w kill yōm bḥiss innu fī shi nāʔiṣ juwwēti. Lamma fattēt awwal marra ʕal-maṭār, kint fakkart innha riḥle ʔaṣīra — btrūḥ w btirjaʕ. Bas il-ayyām ṣārit shhūr, wish-shhūr snīn, w inta ʕam tinsa shakl shwāriʕ baldak. Aṣʕab shi huwwe lamma ḥada yisʔalni: mnēn inta? Laʔannu ij-jawāb ma ʕād basīṭ. Ana min Libnān bas ʕāyish hōn. Libnāni bil-ʔalb bas ḥayāti hōn. Bil-lēl, bismaʕ Fayrūz w bḥiss innu ṣōta ʕam yisḥabni ʕal-wara — ʕa-Jabal Ṣannīn, ʕa-Sāḥit ish-Shuhadā, ʕa-rīḥit il-manga b-Ṣayda. Biktub shiʕr bil-lēl ʕan balad ma ʕād byishbih il-balad yalli taraktu. Baʕrif innu Libnān tghayar, w ana kamēn tghayart. Bas il-ḥanīn ma byitghayar — hayda ish-shi il-waḥīd ith-thābit b-ḥayāti.
english: It's been ten years since I left Lebanon, and every day I feel something is missing inside me. When I first entered the airport, I thought it was a short trip — you go and come back. But the days became months, and the months years, and you start forgetting what the streets of your country look like. The hardest thing is when someone asks me: Where are you from? Because the answer is no longer simple. I'm from Lebanon but I live here. Lebanese at heart but my life is here. At night, I listen to Fairouz and I feel her voice pulling me back — to Mount Sannine, to Martyrs' Square, to the smell of mangoes in Sidon. I write poetry at night about a country that no longer resembles the country I left. I know Lebanon changed, and I changed too. But the longing never changes — that's the only constant thing in my life.

comprehensionQuestions.question: How long has the narrator been away from Lebanon?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Ten years (عشر سنين)

comprehensionQuestions.question: What is the hardest question for the narrator?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Where are you from? — because the answer is no longer simple

comprehensionQuestions.question: What does the narrator do at night?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Listens to Fairouz and writes poetry about Lebanon

comprehensionQuestions.question: What does the narrator say never changes?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: The longing / nostalgia (الحنين ما بيتغيّر)

title: Understanding the News
arabic: فَتَحت التِلفزيون وكان المُذيع عَم يحكي عَن الوَضع الإقتصادي بِلبنان. قال إنّو سِعر الدولار رِجِع إرتَفَع، وإنّو الناس عَم تشتكي مِن غَلا المَعيشة. وَزير المالِيّة طِلِع عَالتِلفزيون وقال: عَم نِشتغِل عَخُطّة إصلاحِيّة جديدة. بَس الناس بِالشّارِع ما صَدَّقو. واحَد قال: كِل يَوم مَنسمَع وُعود بَس ما مِنشوف نَتيجة. إمرا تانية قالِت: أَنا موَظَّفة بِالدَّولة ومَعاشي ما بيكَفّي خُبز. المُحَلِّل السياسي قال إنّو البلَد بحاجة لإصلاحات جِدِّيّة بِالقِطاع المَصرَفي وبِالإدارة العامّة. ضاف إنّو المُجتَمَع الدَّولي عَم يراقِب الوَضع وعَم يشتِرِط إصلاحات قَبل ما يقَدِّم مُساعَدات. قَفَّلت التِلفزيون وقُلت بقَلبي: يا ريت الأَفعال تِصير قَد الحَكي.
transliteration: Fataḥt it-tilfizyōn w kān il-muzīʕ ʕam yiḥki ʕan il-waḍʕ il-iʔtiṣādi b-Libnān. ʔāl innu siʕr id-dōlār rijiʕ irtafaʕ, w innu in-nās ʕam tishtiki min ghala il-maʕīshe. Wazīr il-māliyye ṭiliʕ ʕat-tilfizyōn w ʔāl: ʕam nishtaghil ʕa-khuṭṭa iṣlāḥiyye jdīde. Bas in-nās bish-shāriʕ ma ṣaddaʔu. Wāḥad ʔāl: kill yōm mnismaʕ wuʕūd bas ma mnshūf natīje. Imra tānye ʔālit: ana mwaẓẓafe bid-dawle w maʕāshi ma byikaffi khubz. Il-muḥallil is-siyāsi ʔāl innu il-balad b-ḥāje la-iṣlāḥāt jiddiyye bil-ʔiṭāʕ il-maṣrafi w bil-idāra il-ʕāmme. Ḍāf innu il-mujtamaʕ id-dawli ʕam yrāʔib il-waḍʕ w ʕam yishtiriṭ iṣlāḥāt ʔabil ma yʔaddim musāʕadāt. ʔaffalt it-tilfizyōn w ʔult b-ʔalbi: ya rēt il-afʕāl tṣīr ʔadd il-ḥaki.
english: I turned on the TV and the anchor was talking about the economic situation in Lebanon. He said the dollar price went up again, and that people are complaining about the high cost of living. The Minister of Finance appeared on TV and said: We are working on a new reform plan. But people on the street didn't believe it. One man said: Every day we hear promises but we don't see results. Another woman said: I'm a government employee and my salary isn't enough for bread. The political analyst said the country needs serious reforms in the banking sector and in public administration. He added that the international community is watching the situation and requiring reforms before providing aid. I turned off the TV and said to myself: I wish actions would match the words.

comprehensionQuestions.question: What was the news broadcast about?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: The economic situation in Lebanon and the rising dollar price

comprehensionQuestions.question: What did the Minister of Finance promise?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: A new reform plan

comprehensionQuestions.question: What did the government employee say?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Her salary isn't enough for bread

comprehensionQuestions.question: What does the narrator wish at the end?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: That actions would match the words (يا ريت الأفعال تصير قد الحكي)
